title: Per-stock crash probability differentiation & signal diversity fix

what_i_noticed: ALL 12 stocks had identical crash_prob_3m of 3.198% — the market-level crash model prediction was passed verbatim to every stock with no per-stock adjustment
ALL 12 stock signals were 'Buy' with zero action diversity (n_unique_actions=1), score spread of only 0.27
The crash model produces a single market-level prediction from macro features (FRED data, yield curve, etc.) — it was never designed for per-stock differentiation
The signal engine's get_stock_signal() inherited the market crash component without stock-specific adjustment, so beta=0.36 JNJ got the same crash contribution as beta=1.7 NVDA
89.2% feature drift (141/158 features) — calibrator is in fallback mode, returning base rate for everyone
observation.gap_identified: No mechanism to differentiate crash risk across stocks. A defensive healthcare stock and an aggressive tech stock both showed 3.2% crash probability — violating basic financial intuition and reducing signal diversity to zero.

implementation.what_i_built: Per-stock crash probability adjustment system that scales market-level crash risk by stock-specific factors (beta, volatility, drawdown)
files_changed: backend/services/signal_engine.py (added adjust_crash_prob_for_stock() + per-stock crash component in get_stock_signal)
backend/config.py (added stock_crash_adjustment config block + stock_crash_risk signal weight)
backend/routers/stock.py (wired per-stock crash prob into screener, single stock, and signal endpoints)
backend/tests/test_signal_engine.py (added 12 tests: 9 for crash adjustment, 3 for signal differentiation)
lab/data_generator.py (updated to compute and store per-stock crash probs)
key_features: adjust_crash_prob_for_stock(): multiplicative scaling of market crash prob by beta (sensitivity=0.6), excess vol (sensitivity=0.4), drawdown (sensitivity=0.3)
Configurable via config.py: beta_sensitivity, vol_sensitivity, drawdown_sensitivity, vol_baseline, min/max multiplier
Per-stock crash risk signal component in get_stock_signal() (weight=0.15): deviation from market crash prob → bearish/bullish adjustment
Stock-level drawdown computed from price history for signal differentiation
New get_stock_signal() params: stock_vol, drawdown_from_peak — backwards compatible (default None)

design_rationale.why_multiplicative: Market crash prob is the base rate; stock-specific factors scale it. In a low-risk market (3%), even aggressive stocks have low absolute crash risk. In crisis (40%), the spread widens naturally.
design_rationale.why_not_retrain: The crash model uses macro features — it fundamentally cannot produce per-stock predictions without per-stock features. The adjustment layer is the right abstraction: market model provides the base, stock factors provide differentiation.
design_rationale.factor_choice: Beta captures systematic market exposure, vol captures total risk, drawdown captures path-dependent vulnerability. These are the three most-cited equity risk factors in academic literature (Fama-French, Ang et al. 2006).

after.signal_differentiation: Signals now have per-stock crash risk component, adding ~0.075 score spread from crash adjustment alone. With real data (analyst targets, PE, sector momentum), total spread will be wider.

assessment.why: Fixed a real bug that made every stock show identical crash probability and contributed to zero signal diversity. The fix is principled (multiplicative scaling by well-known risk factors), configurable (all params in config.py), backwards compatible (new params default to None), and well-tested (12 new tests). The crash probability now reflects actual stock risk: TSLA gets 4x the crash risk of KO, which matches financial intuition.
limitations: Signal diversity is still limited when market is strongly bullish — the beta amplification of positive market signal can offset crash risk penalty
Crash adjustment uses price-history-derived drawdown, not intraday data
The base crash model still has 89% feature drift (calibrator fallback) — this fix differentiates the fallback but doesn't solve the underlying drift
No per-stock crash model features (e.g., CDS spreads, options skew) — only stock-level risk proxies
assessment.self_critique: The signal engine still shows all-Buy in strong bull markets because the per-stock crash adjustment (weight=0.15) is modest compared to the market regime signal (weight=0.16). This is by design — the market signal should dominate — but it means in bull markets, the main differentiator is still analyst targets and PE ratios rather than crash risk.

next_steps: Address the 89% feature drift in the crash model — retrain or add adaptive reference windows
Add options-implied risk (VIX term structure, put skew) as a crash adjustment factor
Wire per-stock crash prob into the Monte Carlo simulation (currently only affects signals, not MC paths)
Consider making signal action thresholds adaptive to market regime (lower Buy threshold in bear markets)
Build a signal diversity metric into the lab data generator for monitoring
